LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION'S MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO FILE AND SERVE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THEY WILL BE PREJUDICED IF PLAINTIFF IS PERMITTED TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE TRIAL DATE IS APPROXIMATELY EIGHT MONTHS AWAY AND THE PROPOSED ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE OUT OF THE SAME TRANSACTION AND OCCURRENCES SET FORTH IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHANNEL PARK DEVELOPMENT, LLC, THE RIDING GROUP II, LLC, SIGNATURE PROPERTIES,INC., AND DEVCON CONSTRUCTION'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. LIBERTY MUTUAL INS. CO. V. BROOKFIELD CRYSTAL COVE LLC. (2013) 219 CAL.APP.4TH 98, DOES NOT REQUIRE PLAINTIFF TO DELINEATE BETWEEN THE DEFECTS THAT CAUSES ACTUAL DAMAGES AND THE DEFECTS THAT HAVE NOT CAUSED ACTUAL DAMAGE AT THE PLEADING STAGE. DEFENDANTS CAN REASONABLY DETERMINE WHAT ISSUES MUST BE ADMITTED OR DENIED, OR WHAT COUNTS OR CLAIMS ARE DIRECTED AGAINST THEM. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612.) DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: THOMAS PURTELL, ESQ., AND EMMA NELSON-MUNSON, ESQ., 415.437.1800, FOR PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT 255 BAERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION.
JEFFREY GOLDMAN, ESQ., 949.567.3547, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT KAWNEER COMPANY, INC.
BENJAMIN KOEGEL, ESQ., 916.367.7098, FOR DEFENDANTS/CROSS COMPLAINANTS CHANNEL PARK DEVELOPMENT, LLC, THE RIDING GROUP II, LLC, SIGNATURE PROPERTIES,INC. and DEVCON CONSTRUCTION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs CHANNEL PARK DEVELOPMENT, LLC, THE RIDING GROUP II, LLC, SIGNATURE PROPERTIES,INC., and DEVCON CONSTRUCTION's DEMURRER IS overruled as to all causes of action. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Brookfield Crystal Cove LLC. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 98, does not require Plaintiff to delineate between the defects that causes actual damages and the defects that have not caused actual damage at the pleading stage. Defendants can reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against them. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Seeking Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: THOMAS PURTELL, ESQ., 415.437.1800, FOR PLAINTIFF 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION'S Motion Seeking Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint IS Granted. Defendants have not established that they will be prejudiced if Plaintiff is permitted to file an amended complaint. The trial date is approximately eight months away and the proposed additional causes of action arise out of the same transaction and occurrences set forth in the original complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
